_comment: Semaglutide HbA1c dose-response in T2D, 6 SUSTAIN Phase 3 RCTs. Per-arm change-from-baseline + dispersion extracted from AACT snapshot 2026-04-12 (outcome_measurements.txt). AACT primary HbA1c outcome IDs cover all arms in a single registered outcome per trial. For LSM ± SE outcomes (SUSTAIN-2/4/5/7), SD is computed via sd = se * sqrt(n). For MEAN ± SD outcomes (SUSTAIN-1, SUSTAIN-FORTE) the AACT raw SD is used directly; _aact_se is then back-computed as sd/sqrt(n) for engine bookkeeping. For active-comparator trials (SUSTAIN-2 vs sitagliptin, SUSTAIN-4 vs insulin glargine, SUSTAIN-7 vs dulaglutide) the active comparator arm is dropped per Option A (Round 3 SURPASS pattern); engine treats the lowest semaglutide dose (0.5 mg) as the per-trial reference, comparator info preserved in trial-level metadata. For placebo-controlled trials (SUSTAIN-1, SUSTAIN-5) placebo is the dose=0 reference. SUSTAIN-FORTE is a head-to-head semaglutide 2 mg vs 1 mg (no placebo); 1 mg is the per-trial reference. All 6 PMIDs verified via PubMed MCP get_article_metadata on 2026-05-14 (no PMID guessing). One user-supplied bibliographic detail was incorrect: the user listed SUSTAIN-FORTE as Lancet 2021;398:172-185, but PubMed confirms the correct citation is Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2021;9(9):563-574, PMID 34293304, DOI 10.1016/S2213-8587(21)00174-1.
source: Pooled semaglutide HbA1c dose-response, 6 SUSTAIN Phase 3 trials (AACT 2026-04-12)
dose_assignment_method: as-randomized per-trial dose (0.5, 1.0, 2.0 mg semaglutide subcutaneous once weekly)
class_equivalence_caveat: All 6 trials use the SAME molecule (semaglutide) and SAME route (subcutaneous once-weekly), so the pooled slope is a within-drug dose-response curve. Trials differ in background therapy (monotherapy, metformin, metformin+thiazolidinediones, metformin+/-sulfonylurea, basal insulin +/- metformin) and follow-up window (30, 40, or 56 weeks). These design differences contribute to between-trial heterogeneity in the dose-response but do not violate dose-equivalence.
active_comparator_handling: Option A: active comparator arms (sitagliptin 100 mg in SUSTAIN-2; insulin glargine titrated in SUSTAIN-4; dulaglutide 0.75 mg and 1.5 mg in SUSTAIN-7) are excluded from the dose-response arms list. For these 3 trials, the lowest semaglutide dose (0.5 mg) is the engine's per-trial reference (is_reference=true). Comparator information is recorded in trial-level metadata fields comparator_label, comparator_lsm_change_pct, comparator_dispersion, comparator_n. SUSTAIN-FORTE (1 mg vs 2 mg head-to-head with no placebo) uses 1 mg as its per-trial reference; the linear pool sees this trial's reference mean ≠ 0 baseline change, which the engine handles correctly because each trial's contrasts are computed relative to its own reference inside fitLinear/fitRCS.
estimand_uniformity: Treatment-regimen / treatment-policy estimand used consistently across all 6 trials for cross-trial comparability (same convention as the SURPASS fixture, Round 3). For SUSTAIN-1, SUSTAIN-2, SUSTAIN-4, SUSTAIN-5, SUSTAIN-7 the AACT-registered primary outcome is the MMRM treatment-policy estimand and is used directly. For SUSTAIN-FORTE, AACT records both 'on-treatment without rescue medication' (= trial-product estimand) and 'in-trial' (= treatment-policy estimand) results for the SAME outcome_id; the trial-product estimand row is used here (mean -2.0 / -2.2 for 1 / 2 mg) because the abstract head-line and FDA labelling pivot on it; the in-trial estimand (-1.9 / -2.2) is also AACT-recorded and the per-arm Δ from the two estimands differs by ≤0.1 percentage points (smaller than the cross-trial heterogeneity).
dispersion_handling: AACT mixes two dispersion conventions across the 6 SUSTAIN trials: (a) LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN ± Standard Error (MMRM analysis) for SUSTAIN-2/4/5/7; (b) MEAN ± Standard Deviation (raw observed change-from-baseline) for SUSTAIN-1 and SUSTAIN-FORTE. For (a) the engine input 'sd' field is computed via sd = se × sqrt(n), with _aact_se preserved as the raw SE for audit. For (b) the AACT raw SD goes directly into the 'sd' field, with _aact_se back-computed as sd/sqrt(n) for documentation. Engine mdCovariance() uses sd^2/n which is invariant to this re-expression, so both branches produce identical contrast variances.
se_to_sd_conversion: AACT-reported SE converted to SD via sd = se * sqrt(n) using the per-arm analyzed N from outcome_counts.txt. This SD represents the within-arm SD of the change-from-baseline LSM (NOT the residual model SD); for WLS engine use this is the correct quantity because variance(LSM) = SE^2 = SD^2 / N.
pubmed_retrieval_notes: All 6 PMIDs verified via PubMed MCP (get_article_metadata) on 2026-05-14. According to PubMed: SUSTAIN-1 (PMID 28110911, doi:10.1016/S2213-8587(17)30013-X, Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2017;5(4):251-260, Sorli C et al.); SUSTAIN-2 (PMID 28385659, doi:10.1016/S2213-8587(17)30092-X, Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2017;5(5):341-354, Ahrén B et al.); SUSTAIN-4 (PMID 28344112, doi:10.1016/S2213-8587(17)30085-2, Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2017;5(5):355-366, Aroda VR et al.); SUSTAIN-5 (PMID 29688502, doi:10.1210/jc.2018-00070, J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2018;103(6):2291-2301, Rodbard HW et al.); SUSTAIN-7 (PMID 29397376, doi:10.1016/S2213-8587(18)30024-X, Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2018;6(4):275-286, Pratley RE et al.); SUSTAIN-FORTE (PMID 34293304, doi:10.1016/S2213-8587(21)00174-1, Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2021;9(9):563-574, Frías JP et al. — NOTE: user-supplied citation of Lancet 2021;398:172-185 was incorrect; PubMed-verified citation is Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol, not Lancet).

trials.population: Treatment-naive adults with T2DM on diet/exercise alone; baseline HbA1c 8.05% (SD 0.85)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211134754, time_frame 'Week 0, week 30'). Dispersion is MEAN ± Standard Deviation (param_type=MEAN, dispersion_type='Standard Deviation') for all 3 result_groups. Two placebo arms (volume-matched to sema 0.5 and to sema 1.0) were pre-specified as POOLED for analysis (per Sorli 2017 abstract and result_group OG002 description); the pooled placebo is the dose=0 reference here. AACT-registered values: sema 0.5 mg -1.47 (SD 1.02, n=128), sema 1.0 mg -1.56 (SD 1.26, n=130), pooled placebo -0.00 (SD 0.90, n=129). Abstract reports -1.45/-1.55/-0.02 (rounded to 2 dp on the LSM, slightly different second-decimal from the AACT MEAN value; AACT values used for cross-trial uniformity).

trials.comparator_label: Sitagliptin 100 mg oral once daily (pooled across two placebo-arms-of-semaglutide strata)
trials.background_therapy: metformin, thiazolidinediones, or both (stable pre-trial dose)
trials.population: Adults with T2DM inadequately controlled on metformin, TZD, or both; baseline HbA1c 8.1% (SD 0.93)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211054127, time_frame 'Week 0, week 56'). LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN ± Standard Error for all 3 result_groups. Two sitagliptin-strata (paired with sema-placebo 0.5 and sema-placebo 1.0) were pre-specified as POOLED for analysis; pooled sitagliptin arm dropped per Option A. Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt. Per-arm means match Ahrén Lancet Diab Endo 2017 abstract (sema 0.5 1.3, sema 1.0 1.6 — both 1-dp rounded to AACT 2-dp -1.32/-1.61).

trials.comparator_label: Insulin glargine SC once daily, titrated to pre-breakfast SMPG 4.0-5.5 mmol/L (open-label)
trials.population: Insulin-naive adults with T2DM uncontrolled on metformin ± sulfonylurea; baseline HbA1c 8.17% (SD 0.89)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211535270, time_frame 'Week 0, week 30'). LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN ± Standard Error for all 3 result_groups. Insulin glargine arm dropped per Option A. Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt (362/360/360). Per-arm means match Aroda Lancet Diab Endo 2017 abstract exactly (-1.21 / -1.64 for sema 0.5 / 1.0).

trials.population: Adults with T2DM uncontrolled on basal insulin ± metformin; baseline HbA1c 8.4% (67.9 mmol/mol)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=210959194, time_frame 'Week 0, week 30'). LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN ± Standard Error for all 3 result_groups. Two volume-matched placebo strata POOLED into a single placebo arm per the protocol-specified analysis. Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt (132/131/133). Per-arm means: sema 0.5 -1.45 (SE 0.09), sema 1.0 -1.85 (SE 0.09), placebo -0.09 (SE 0.09). Abstract reports -1.4 / -1.8 / -0.1 to 1-dp (rounding agrees with AACT 2-dp values).

trials.comparator_label: Dulaglutide 0.75 mg and Dulaglutide 1.5 mg SC once weekly (TWO active-comparator arms, both dropped per Option A)
trials.population: Adults with T2DM on metformin monotherapy; baseline HbA1c 8.2% (SD 0.89)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211482935, time_frame 'Week 0, week 40'). LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN ± Standard Error for all 4 result_groups (sema 0.5, sema 1.0, dula 0.75, dula 1.5). Both dulaglutide arms dropped per Option A; this is a 4-arm trial reduced to 2 semaglutide arms with sema 0.5 mg as the per-trial reference. Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt (301/300/299/299). Per-arm sema means match Pratley Lancet Diab Endo 2018 abstract (-1.5 / -1.8 to 1-dp; AACT 2-dp values -1.51 / -1.78).

trials.comparator_label: Semaglutide 1.0 mg SC once weekly (active comparator; this is the per-trial reference here, dose=1.0 — NOT dropped)
trials.population: Adults with T2DM uncontrolled on metformin ± sulfonylurea, baseline HbA1c 8.9% (SD 0.6, 73.3 mmol/mol)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211056609, time_frame 'Week 0, week 40'). MEAN ± Standard Deviation (param_type=MEAN, dispersion_type='Standard Deviation'). Two estimand categories registered: 'On-treatment without rescue medication' (trial-product estimand, sema 1.0 -2.0/SD 1.0, sema 2.0 -2.2/SD 1.0) AND 'In-trial' (treatment-policy estimand, sema 1.0 -1.9/SD 1.0, sema 2.0 -2.2/SD 1.1). Trial-product estimand row used here (matches Frías Lancet Diab Endo 2021 abstract headline ETD -0.23 ppt). NO placebo arm — 1 mg is the engine's per-trial reference (lowest dose), 2 mg is the contrast. The linear pool sees this trial's reference mean ≠ 0 (it's already -2.0); the engine handles this correctly because each trial's contrasts are computed relative to its own reference within fitLinear/fitRCS. Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt (481 sema 1.0, 480 sema 2.0).
